fundamentals.name: Tower Semiconductor Ltd.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Tower Semiconductor Ltd., an independent semiconductor foundry, provides technology, development, and process platforms for integrated circuits in the United States, Japan, rest of Asia, and Europe. The company provides customizable process technologies, including SiGe, SiPho, mixed signal CMOS, RF CMOS, CMOS image sensor, non-imaging sensors, wafers, and integrated power management. It also offers a design enablement platform for a design cycle, as well as transfer optimization and development process services to integrated device manufacturers and fabless companies. The company serves consumer applications, personal computers, communications, handsets and smartphones, automotive, industrial, aerospace, and medical devices. Tower Semiconductor Ltd. was incorporated in 1993 and is headquartered in Migdal Haemek, Israel

judgment.summary: Strong growth metrics are tempered by a high valuation and significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 15.7%; next earnings date is 2026-08-03.
positives: Revenue growth 15.5% YoY and earnings growth 62.9% YoY indicate strong business expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for current quarter (51.6%) and fiscal year (45.9%) are robust.
Last quarter's EPS beat of 15.7% suggests operational execution.
High current and quick ratios (5.63 and 4.75) indicate strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 111.56 and forward P/E of 42.19 are very high, suggesting a premium valuation.
PEG ratio of 7.35 indicates that expected growth may not justify the current stock price.
Debt/Equity ratio of 5.24 is high, indicating significant reliance on debt financing.
Free cash flow of $128.95M is modest relative to market cap, potentially limiting flexibility.

news.paragraph: Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) is facing mixed signals, with one analyst expressing a bearish view while other reports highlight strong AI growth momentum and significant silicon photonics agreements totaling $1.3 billion. The company recently reported a 15.7% EPS beat for its last quarter and has an analyst growth estimate of 51.6% for the current quarter, suggesting underlying business strength despite some bearish sentiment.
